title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Do I need Term Insurance if I don't have any dependents?
Metadata: Know why to buy term insurance despite not having dependents.
Keywords: term insurance calculator, online term plan
Introduction
While considering different types of life insurance plans, you could have wondered what is term plan? Among the life insurance plans available in the market, term insurance is one of the simplest and most affordable plans. A term plan provides you with a life cover and to receive this,you have to make premium payments to the Insurance Provider. As the name suggests, the term plan is valid for a specified period. This is also called a policy term. If the policyholder passes away during the policy term, the insurance service provider will provide the sum assured as the death benefit to the nominees. However, if a policyholder survives the policy term, no benefits will be paid except in cases where term plan is available with option of return of premium on maturity.
Why Term Insurance?
The main purpose of term insurance is to provide a financial safety net to your family. A pure term insurance plan does not provide any maturity benefit. Suppose you don’t have any dependents in your family. The question then arises is whether you still need term insurance? In the subsequent section, we will discuss some situations where a term insurance plan will provide benefits, despite not having dependents.
Should I get Term Insurance if I don't have dependents?
Here’s a look at the reasons for buying term insurance even if you don’t have dependents:
You can get the advantage of lower premiums: While at present, you might not be having dependents, it is still a wise decision to get term insurance when you are younger. This is because you can enjoy the benefit of lower premiums.Whereas, if you buy a term plan later in life, the premium charges are likely to be higher.
A term insurance plan will provide financial security for your parents: Let’s consider another reason for purchasing a term insurance plan.  You may not have any dependents at the moment butyour parents may retire in near future, it may  be difficult for them to live a financially independent life in case any unfortunate incident happens to you. A term insurance plan will help in providing the financial requirements of your elderly parents in your absence.
A term insurance plan will help to meet your liabilities: During your lifetime, you could have incurred liabilities, such as a home loan, car loan, personal loan and so on. A term insurance plan will help your family or parents meet these liabilities with ease, in your absence. Your family or parents will not be under mental stress to pay for these liabilities, nor would they lose the assets, such as car, home etc for meeting these liabilities.
Tax benefits: If you purchase a term insurance policy, you can claim tax deductions on premium payments, of up to a maximum of  Rs. 1.5 Lakhs each year, under Section 80 C of the Income Tax Act 1961 (the Act). The death benefit is also exempt from tax under Section 10 (10) D of the Act. The above tax benefits are subject to the provisions stated therein
How to buy Term Insurance Online?
An online term plan is simply a plan that can be purchased online. As compared to an offline plan, it provides multiple benefits, such as the option to compare the benefits and features of various plans and the convenient and hassle-free application process. You also don’t need to physically visit a branch or contact an agent.
Here’s a look at the steps involved in buying term insurance online:
Step 1: Visit the official website of the insurance service provider.
Step 2: Select the term insurance plan by comparing different variants.  Next, fill in the requisite details. You must ensure that you have provided the complete and correct details.
Step 3: Submit the filled-in form along with the documents, if required.
Step 4: Now make the premium payment online using digital payment methods, such as net banking, credit card, debit card and so on.
Before purchasing an online term plan, you can zero in on the right amount of coverage, using the term insurance calculator. You can know the amount of premium payment– required to meet your family’s life goals– beforehand using this free calculator. It can easily help you assess whether the term plan is within your budget.
Conclusion
Thus, irrespective of the fact that you don’t have dependents, you may  purchase a term insurance plan. It will provide an adequate financial cover, if you choose to start a family later. This plan will also act as a financial safety net for your elderly parents. Remember, a term insurance provides a financial cushion to your family against life risks. If you have not opted for a term insurance plan, then your family may  experience   financial consequences, in the case of an unfortunate eventuality. Also, consider purchasing a term insurance plan early in your life so that you have to pay lesser premium throughout life.

Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
